  Regulatory Disclosures
Regulatory Disclosures
At Union Bank of India, we are dedicated to transparency and informed
    financial decisions. Our Basel III Disclosures page offers a comprehensive
    insight into our adherence to global banking standards and regulatory
    requirements. Here, you can access detailed information on our capital
    adequacy, risk management practices, and other essential disclosures that
    ensure the stability and security of our operations. An informed customer is
    an empowered customer, and this page is designed to provide you with the
    knowledge you need to make sound financial choices. Explore our Basel III
    Disclosures and gain a deeper understanding of our commitment to financial
    integrity and regulatory compliance.

text:
You will be redirected to another website domain. Do you want to continue?
Proceed
Cancel

General >
  Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
INTRODUCTION
Union Bank of India recognizes the expectations of its customers and the visitors to its website
with regard to privacy, confidentiality and security of their personal information which we
receive while browsing/ using our website/ services. Keeping Your personal information secure
and using it solely for activities related to Bank’s business is a top priority of the Bank.
OBJECTIVE
We have taken adequate measures aimed at protecting the personal information entrusted and
disclosed to us. This policy (Policy) is framed to inform you the privacy practice followed & the
governing way in which the Bank collects, uses, discloses, stores, secures and disposes your
personal information and sensitive personal data or information. You are requested to go through
the contents herein and If you do not agree with the practices mentioned herein, you may opt
out for using this website or using our services. However, your continued browsing/ using this
website, shall be your deemed acceptance of the Policy.
Definitions
“Bank” shall mean Union Bank of India having its Registered Office at Union Bank Bhavan, 239,
Vidhan Bhavan Marg, Nariman Point, Mumbai –400 021, Maharashtra, India and in terms of this
Policy and it shall include the words “we”, “us”, “UBI” which are used interchangeably in this
Policy.
“Data” means a representation of information, facts, concepts, opinions or instructions in a
manner suitable for communication, interpretation or processing by manually or by automated
means;
“Person” includes— (a) an individual; (b) a Hindu Undivided Family; (c) a company; (d) a firm;
(e) an association of persons or a body of individuals, whether incorporated or not; (f) the State;
and (g) every artificial juristic person, not falling within any of the preceding sub-clauses;
“Personal Data” or “Personal information” means any data or information which allows the User
to be identified directly or indirectly and shall include “Sensitive personal data or information”.
Provided that, any personal data or information that is freely available or accessible in public
domain or furnished under the right to information act, 2005 or any other law for the time
being in force shall not be regarded as sensitive personal data or information for the purposes
of this policy.
“Processing” in relation to personal data or information means an automated operation or set of
operations performed on personal data, and may include operations such as collection,
recording, organisation, structuring, storage, adaptation, alteration, retrieval, use, alignment
or combination, indexing, sharing, disclosure by transmission, dissemination or otherwise making
available, restriction, erasure or destruction;
“Publicly available information” shall mean any information or data of the user which Bank
reasonably believes is lawfully publicly available. All other information or data which is not
publicly available shall be Non publicly available information for the purpose of this Policy.
“Sensitive personal data or information” of a User means such personal information which
consists of information relating to:
financial information such as Bank account or credit card or debit card or other payment
instrument details
medical records & history;
biometric information
Other government-issued identification number such as country-identification
“User” shall mean such Persons who are using the Bank’s services or the Bank’s website and to
whom this Policy is applicable, and it shall include the words “you”, “customer” which are used
interchangeably in this Policy.
Applicability
This Policy is applicable with reference to the Personal Data or Personal Information of the User
which is non publicly available, processing of which is done by or for the UBI whether manually
or mechanically including automated means. Any information that is freely available or
accessible in public domain or furnished under the RTI Act 2005 or any other law for the time
being in force shall not be regarded as personal information for the purpose of this Policy.
1. Mode of collecting Information of Users
Bank will collect and process Your personal data provided through application forms, portals,
mobile apps, devices like ATM and data collected from public domain, etc. which are necessary
in order to provide our services to You such as your identification, requirements.
We may collect information about You from other sources as mentioned below, and after
ascertaining the genuineness of the same may add it to the Data we already hold and use in
the manner described in this Privacy Policy.
If data is collected from third-party service providers to provide relevant marketing, offers,
services to you, the same would be used & processed with your consent only. The third-party
service providers include email service providers, public data bases, social media sites as well
as other third parties as deemed appropriate. We including our service providers may monitor,
record & retain your image, conversations and/ or electronic communication between you &
us electronically or otherwise as deemed appropriate.
If data is collected from Credit Information agencies/companies, risk management and fraud
prevention agencies, national & government data bases, the same would be retained & used
as deemed legally appropriate
2. Information we process about You includes
Personal details such as name, address, signatures, date of birth, father’s name, mother’s
name, email address, nationality, marital status, gender, your occupation, education and
qualifications, annual income, and source of income and mobile numbers.
Copies of photographs, permanent residential address proof, viz. visa, passport, driving
license, utility bills, bank statements, national ID cards. PAN card, Aadhar Card, Credit/Debit
card and bank account statement.
Transaction details
Financial information/objectives and experience,
Voice/biometric may also be used for enrolment (or) authentication purposes.
Details of nominee, witnesses, guardians which includes their addresses and relationship
with you.
CCTV footage and other information obtained through electronic means.
IP addresses, Device used in relation to Banking services, cookies, geolocations
3. Lawful grounds for processing Personal Information
Bank will process your personal data in compliance with applicable data privacy laws in India
by relying on one or more of the following lawful grounds:
Consent - You have explicitly agreed to our processing for a specific reason
Performance of a Contract - The processing is necessary to perform the agreement we have
with you
Legal Obligation - The processing is necessary for compliance with legal obligations under
certain laws
Legitimate Interest - The processing is necessary for the purposes of a legitimate interest
pursued by the Bank
4. Purpose of Collection and Use of Personal Information
The Bank collects and uses the financial information and other personal information from
User. This information is collected and used for specific business purposes or for other
related purposes designated by the Bank or for a lawful purpose to comply with the
applicable laws and regulations. The Bank shall not divulge any personal information
collected from the customer, for cross selling or any other purposes outside the Bank. The
authenticity of the personal information provided by the customer shall not be the
responsibility of the Bank. The processing of the data may be done by us or for us in relation
to any of the following purposes:
To ensure that our customer accounts are well-managed.
To provide requested information & statements about your account/s
To resolve complaints & enquiries.
To administer, manage, evaluate, develop & improvement in our services in relation to
your account, products & services availed or propose to be availed
To personalize your experience with us by presenting products & offers tailored/ suitable
to you
To keep you informed about products and services you hold with us and send you
information about products or services (including those of our subsidiaries/JV) which may
be of interest to you.
For debt collection & related services.
To comply with RBI, regulatory obligations and reporting duties including to prevent,
detect, investigate, and prosecute fraud and alleged fraud, money laundering and other
crimes. Bank may record CCTV image as and when you visit the Branch/ATM.
Assess application/s submitted by you to carry out fraud/Money Laundering/ identity &
screening and other mandatory regulatory checks.
Monitoring/recording of communications including phone calls to further improve
services.
To share information with relevant authorities, including RBI/Income Tax and other
government authorities, credit reference agencies, fraud prevention agencies and India
and overseas regulators and authorities.
To share your information with our partners & service providers. When we ask for your
consent, we will provide you with more information on how we will use your data based
on your consent.
For our business purposes such as data analysis, audits, developing/ improving our
products & services
5. Disclosure of Personal Information
The personal information collected by the Bank shall not be disclosed to any other
organization except:
where the disclosure has been agreed in a written contract or otherwise between the Bank
and the customer
where the Bank is required to disclose the personal information to a third party on a need-
to-know basis, provided that in such case the Bank shall inform such third party of the
confidential nature of the personal information and shall keep the same standards of
information/ data security as that of the Bank.
As permitted and required by Law.
6. Reasonable Security Practices and Procedures
The security of personal information is a priority and is protected by maintaining physical,
electronic, and procedural safeguards that meet applicable laws. The Bank shall take
reasonable steps and measures to protect the security of the customer’s personal
information from misuse and loss, un-authorized access, modification or disclosure. The
Bank maintains its security systems to ensure that the personal information of the customer
is appropriately protected and follows the extant standard encryption norms followed for
the transmission of information. The Bank ensures that its employees and affiliates respect
the confidentiality of any personal information held by the Bank.
7. Sharing of Information
To help us provide/ improve services, your data may be processed internally and externally
by other third parties. We may use third parties for processing, servicing, monitoring of your
data. We may outsource some services to third parties having expertise in performing the
required processing activities ensuring no drop in the service standard. However, the data
transfers would take place after signing of data transfer agreements incorporating due
safeguards. Due to the size and complexity of our operations, it is not possible for us to name
each of our data recipients in this notice.
8. Data Retention
We will keep the information in our systems or with third parties for as long as necessary to
fulfil the purposes we collected it for, including for the purposes of satisfying any legal,
accounting, or reporting requirements. or as required to comply with any legal obligations
to which we are subject to.
9. Rights available to You
You are entitled to the following rights in respect of your personal data:
Right to Access: You may request to access your data provided by you (or) processed by us.
This enables you to receive a copy of the personal data we hold about you and to check that
we are lawfully processing it.
Right to correction, completion, modification or erasure of personal data: You may request to
correct/update/amend your Personal Data, particularly if found to be inaccurate. It is
important that the personal data we hold about you is accurate and current. Please keep us
informed if your personal data changes during your working relationship with us.
Right to restrict processing/Right to be forgotten You may request object the processing of
your Personal Data, if you think the Bank does not have the right to process it, which would
be subject to effective regulatory/statutory requirements.
Object to Automated Decision Making: You can withdraw a given consent at any time,
without affecting the lawfulness of earlier processing based on consent before such
withdrawal in specific cases. This is subject to legal and regulatory requirement for retention
of such data.
If you have comments, queries relating this Privacy Notice, please contact our Data Protection Officer (DPO) via email or write us at the address below
Address:
Union Bank of India building, 2nd Floor, Data Protection Office, Military Road, Marol, Andheri East, Mumbai – 400059
Email:
dpoffice@unionbankofindia.bank.in
Upon receipt of such a request, Bank shall ensure that the requests are addressed in a timely
manner and in compliance with the applicable laws, regulations and policies. If we
determine that the request cannot be fulfilled, the bank will respond with a reasonable
justification for denial of such request. We will maintain records of all such requests and
any such statements from you, irrespective of their status of processing.
10. Cookie policy
Union Bank of India’s digital platforms use various third party analytical tools. These tools
use cookies which are downloaded to your device when you visit a website in order to provide
a personalized browsing experience. Cookies are used for lots of tasks like remembering your
preferences & settings, provide personalized browsing experience and analyze site
operations. These cookies collect information about how users use a website, for instance,
how often visited pages. All information collected by third party cookies is aggregated and
anonymous. By using our website user/s agree that these types of cookies can be placed on
his/her device. User/s is free to disable/delete these cookies by changing his/her device /
browser settings. Union Bank of India is not responsible for cookies placed in the device of
user/s by any other website and information collected by them.
11. Notice of change
The Bank may, from time to time, change this Policy. The effective date of this Policy, as
stated below, indicates the last time this Policy was revised or materially changed.
Effective Date
22/08/2023

DIVAA ICON Credit Card
Across India, women are shaping households, businesses, communities, and the digital economy with unmatched energy and ambition. Recognizing this powerful shift, Union Bank of India proudly upgrades its women centric credit card proposition with the launch of the DIVAA ICON Credit Card a premium offering crafted to celebrate the modern woman’s lifestyle, aspirations, and financial independence.

PM Svanidhi credit card
The PM SVANidhi Scheme has played a vital role in uplifting street vendors (SVs) by extending micro-credit assistance that supports their livelihoods and business growth. Under the current term loan (TL) model, vendors repay through fixed monthly instalments over a defined period, which has enabled many to strengthen their operations. As businesses evolve, street vendors often encounter new opportunities and requirements. To further empower them, a RuPay Credit Card facility is being introduced for PM SVANidhi beneficiaries. This initiative will provide vendors with on-demand access to credit, offering greater flexibility to meet day-to-day expenses and adapt to changing business needs. By complementing the existing loan structure, the Credit Card ensures that vendors can continue to grow with confidence and agility.

﻿﻿
General >
  Accessible Banking
Accessible Banking
Union Bank of India: Banking for Everyone
At Union Bank of India, we are committed to banking without barriers. We strive to create an inclusive and accessible banking environment where every customer  including persons with diverse abilities and senior citizens  can manage their finances with ease, dignity, and confidence. Through continuous innovation, we aim to ensure our services meet the highest standards of accessibility and user empowerment.
Our Accessibility Promise
We are deeply committed to making Union Bank of India the
most accessible and inclusive bank
for all our customers and employees. This commitment is embedded in our operations, from developing innovative solutions like the
industry-first Talking ATM for Visually Impaired and Braille debit card
to continually enhancing accessibility of our digital platforms. Our dedicated accessibility program ensures we are always improving your banking experience.
How We Make Banking Accessible for You?
We recognize that every individual may have unique accessibility needs. To address this, a range of inclusive products and services has been made available.
These offerings have been designed to support diverse requirements related to vision, hearing, mobility, and overall usability—ensuring that services can be accessed and used by all.
Talking ATM
As pioneers in India, we have deployed thousands of Talking ATMs providing audio guidance for secure and independent transactions.
Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card
The customized card with tactile indicators help you easily identify your card. We offer Debit Card with Touch and Feel Features Sparsh Braille Debit Card, a specially designed debit card for visually impaired customers.
Internet Banking
The Internet Banking Integrated with accessibility features and keyboard operability for screen reader users to enjoy enhanced usability. We have dedicated accessibility page on the home page to guide you on navigation with assistive technologies.
Mobile Banking (ease App)
The mobile banking is integrated with accessibility features, ensuring seamless navigation for screen reader users. Large fonts, clear headings, and intuitive interfaces enhance usability for all.
Digital Rupee App by UBI
The CBDC platform enabled app,  Digital Rupee by UBI incorporates a dedicated “Accessibility” option to provide navigation guidance tailored to the needs of visually impaired users.
Union Virtual Connect (UVConn)
Our WhatsApp chat-based banking service allows you to interact with us through text, offering greater convenience for those with hearing disabilities, available in seven languages, anytime, anywhere.
SMS Banking
Receive instant transaction notifications and avail services like balance inquiries and mini statements via SMS.
Door Step Banking
For senior citizens (above 70 years) and differently-abled persons, we provide essential banking services at your home, ensuring convenience and comfort.
ATMs & Branches locator
Use our
Branch Locator
and
ATM Locator
to find nearest ATMs and branches location near you.
Call Centre (IVR based)
Our 24x7x365 multi-language call center is available for your banking needs.
Union Dial
A quick and convenient way to apply for loan products. All you need to is to just dial the unique number 9619333333 or SMS 	<ULOAN> to 56161 After your call/ SMS received our executive will call you and inform you the scheme guidelines and help you to apply for the loan.
Union Bank of India Website
Our corporate website is designed with comprehensive accessibility features, ensuring ease of information access for all types of users.
NDFDC Loan Support
We are one of the channelling banks for
National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) loans.
We provide NDFDC loans for Self-employment, Education loan, Housing loan, Modified Scooter/Bike loan, Four-wheeler loan & loan for assistive device to any Indian Citizen with 40% or more disability (Disability as defined in PwD Act 2016 or its amendments).
Important Resources
Services offered to visually challenged persons
IBA Banker’s Guide (PDF file)
User Guides for Accessible Services
We provide essential information and user manuals in formats compatible with screen readers and other assistive technologies.
Internet Banking usage with JAWS User Guides
Internet Banking usage with JAWS Accessible PDF file
Internet Banking usage with JAWSePub file
Internet Banking usage with JAWS Digital Braille file
Digital Rupee by Union Bank of India Mobile App with Screen Reader
Accessible PDF - User Manual for Visually Impaired for Operating Digital Rupee by Union Bank of India Mobile App with Screen Reader support
Accessible EPUB - User Manual for Visually Impaired for Operating Digital Rupee by Union Bank of India Mobile App with Screen Reader support
Daisy Audio Book - User Manual for Visually Impaired for Operating Digital Rupee by Union Bank of India Mobile App with Screen Reader support
Talking ATM User Guides
Talking ATM User Guide Accessible pdf file
Talking ATM User Guide Digital Braille file
Stories & Videos
Watch our videos to see how our accessible banking services make a real difference in the lives of our customers. Hear testimonials and learn more about our innovative solutions.
Project Union Access | Union Bank of India
Union Sparsh | Union Bank Of India
Testimonial
UV Conn | Union Bank of India
Recognitions for accessible and disabled friendly banking
We are honored by the recognition we've received for our commitment to accessible and inclusive banking. These awards motivate us to continue striving for excellence in serving all our customers.
Confederation of Indian Industry - India Business & Disability Network (CII-IBDN) Awards for Excellence in Disability Inclusion 2026., Best Employer (Digital Accessibility).
SKOCH BFSI Award (2025–26) - Digital Journey for Credit Cards & SPARSH Credit Card for Visually Impaired.
NAB Delhi Sarojini Triloknath National Award 2022 - Best CSR Impact.
NCPEDP-LTIMindtree Helen Keller Award 2022 - Role Model Company for creating employment opportunities for PwDs.
Disability Positive Award 2023 - Best Organization for PwD Inclusion.

Process for Reactivation of Inoperative/Dormant Accounts
Savings/Current Accounts are classified as Inoperative/Dormant, if there is no Customer Induced Transaction (Financial or Non-Financial) or no KYC updation for last 2 Years.
Reactivation of Inoperative/Dormant Accounts-
Customers need to visit the Branch (Base/Non-Base) and submit-
Request for Activation of Inoperative/Dormant Account signed by Customer/s with details like- Account Number, Account Name, Address, Mobile No./Email ID etc.
Copy of Officially Valid Documents to be submitted (Original Documents to be produced for verification purpose).
Passport size latest Photo of customer/s.
In case of Non-Individual Accounts/Current Accounts, Valid Documents of Entity should be submitted, in addition to request for activation of Inoperative/Dormant Account and Officially Valid Documents with Passport size photograph of Authorised Signatory/s.

General >
  Accessibility Statement
Accessibility Statement
The website supports global Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) and Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance published ‘Accessibility Standards and Guidelines’ listed web accessibility features that includes:
Skip to Main Content
: Quick access to the core content on the page is provided without going through repetitive navigation using the keyboard.
Accessibility Options
: Options to change the size of the text, set a color scheme and increase the text spacing are provided.
Headings
: The Web page content is organized using appropriate headings and subheadings that provide a readable structure. H1 indicates the main heading, whereas H2 indicates a subheading.
Titles
: An appropriate name for each Web page is specified that helps you to understand the page content easily.
Screen Reader Accessibility
: The website design and content is compatible with assistive technologies screen readers and screen magnifiers.
Alternate Text
: Brief description of an image is provided for users with visual disability. If you are using a browser that supports only text or have turned off the image display, you can still know what the image is all about by reading the alternate text in absence of an image. In addition, some browsers display the alternate text in the form of a tooltip when the user moves the mouse pointer over the image.
Link Purpose
: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text.
Consistent Navigation Mechanism
: Consistent means of navigation and style of presentation throughout the Website has been incorporated.
Keyboard Support
: The website can be browsed using a keyboard.
Adjust Contrast Scheme
: Feature of dark mode is available for changing the contrast of the web pages.
ARIA
: Use of ARIA tags to help users to understand and navigate effectively.
Union Bank of India is continuously working towards making the website maximum accessible for persons with disabilities. The website is continuously being enhanced with accessibility features, it is tested regularly to ensure conformance with WCAG 2.1 Level AA Success Criteria to the greatest extent possible
If you have any suggestion regarding the accessibility of this website, please write to
unionaccess@unionbankofindia.bank
to enable us to respond by our accessibility team.

Digital Banking
>
App Banking
>
                Digital Rupee
Digital Rupee
Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) – a revolutionary step towards modernizing the financial landscape. CBDC – Digital Rupee (e₹) is a digital form of fiat currency, issued and regulated by the RBI. In this case, Union Bank of India is at the forefront of embracing this cutting-edge digital currency technology.
With e₹, Union Bank of India aims to transform traditional banking and payment systems, offering a secure, efficient, and inclusive financial ecosystem. By leveraging blockchain technology, e₹ enables seamless and instantaneous transactions, eliminating intermediaries and reducing settlement times.
Union Bank of India's e₹ ensures robust security measures, protecting users from fraud and ensuring the integrity of transactions. As an accessible digital currency, e₹ opens doors to financial inclusion, making banking services available to a wider population, including those without traditional bank accounts.
Stay tuned to explore the exciting features, advantages, and possibilities that Union Bank of India's e₹ brings to the financial world. Discover how this innovative digital currency will shape the future of payments, streamline financial operations, and empower individuals and businesses alike.
Download Digital Rupee by UBI:
Digital Rupee (e₹) App by UBI
Digital Rupee (e₹) app by UBI is now available in Pilot mode on Playstore and Appstore to the Union Bank of India customers. Digital Rupee app provides facility of Digital Wallet where you can hold your digital rupee for making payments. Digital rupee wallet offers functionalities like:
Load: This functionality will help the user to load digital currency in their Digital Rupee Wallet.
Send (Transfer): This functionality will help the user to transfer digital currency from their Digital Rupee Wallet to any user having a Digital Rupee Wallet.
Collect (Receive): This functionality will help the user to request for digital currency from any other Digital Rupee Wallet holder.
Redeem: This functionality will help the user to redeem digital currency in their Digital Rupee Wallet and credit the equivalent amount to their linked Bank account.
Merchant Locator
Features
Programmable CBDC
Gallery
Terms and Conditions
Youtube Video
Contact Us
Accessibility
Union Bank of India's e₹ offers a wide range of features that enhance the efficiency, security, and accessibility of digital transactions. Here are some key features of Union Bank of India’s e₹:
Simplicity and Ease of Micro-Payments:
Earlier users were able to make payment in available denomination starting from Rs. 500/- to 50 Paisa and decimal based payments were not available. Now, users can make fractionalized payment up to One Paisa without the necessity of combining “notes & coins” during a transaction.
Instant Transactions:
With the implementation of Value-based transaction model, Digital Rupee has become even faster and allows user to initiate real-time, instant payments and transfers, enabling swift and efficient financial transactions.
Auto Load:
This feature will help the user to seamlessly transfer Digital Rupee up to Rs. 2000/- without initiating an additional step of loading the wallet every time in case the wallet balance is low.
UPI Interoperability:
User can now easily transfer to any Merchant or Person having a valid UPI ID by scanning their UPI QR code. Union Bank of India’s Digital Rupee offers seamless payment experience by enabling interoperability with UPI.
Accessibility:
In line with the Bank’s vision to promote inclusivity and ensure seamless access for all users, including individuals who are visually impaired, “Digital Rupee App by UBI” now incorporates enhanced features designed to enable visually impaired users to perform all Digital Rupee transactions effortlessly. A dedicated “Accessibility” option has been integrated into the Profile section of the app to provide navigation guidance tailored to the needs of visually impaired users.
PIN-less Transactions:
Users can now make quick and secure payments with Digital Rupee by UBI App, with no PIN required for transactions up to ₹500. This convenient feature is available up to three times a day, making everyday purchases faster, simpler, and more efficient.
Programmable CBDC:
An advanced feature aimed at enabling control over the end-use of funds based on purpose bound parameters. User can send digital currency with programmable rules based on Time Validity of Funds, Geo-location of fund consumption, Purpose basis Merchant Category Code (MCC), Acceptance in terms of CBDC wallets Virtual Private Address (VPA).
These features collectively position Union Bank of India's e₹ as a transformative and forward-thinking digital currency that aims to reshape the way we conduct financial transactions in the digital age.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
What is a Digital Rupee?
Digital Rupee or e₹, is India’s Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC). It is the digital form of India’s physical currency, the Rupee (₹). e₹ is issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in digital form and offers features similar to physical cash like convenience of use, guarantee of RBI, finality of settlement, etc. e₹ is stored in the user’s digital wallet and can be used to receive / send money, and / or make payment for transactions, just like any physical ₹ note.
The logo and tagline for India’s CBDC is as under:
How can Digital Rupee be held and used?
e₹ can be held in an e₹ wallet issued by Union Bank of India. One can start using the e₹ wallet by downloading Digital Rupee by UBI app from the Play Store or the App Store and by following the App instructions and linking it to your existing Bank (savings / current) account. The wallet is a digital representation of your physical wallet and e₹ can be withdrawn/ deposited from your existing bank account into this wallet. Payments to merchants can be made by scanning either the CBDC QR code or the UPI QR code available at the respective merchant location.
What is an e₹ Wallet?
e₹ wallet is a digital wallet that can be opened on one’s mobile phone/device. e₹ wallet stores the Digital Rupee, similar to the physical wallet/purse that stores the physical currency. e₹ wallets are being currently provided by banks and non-banks, which are part of the retail CBDC pilot.
Are e₹ wallets safe?
e₹ wallets are safe and secure. There is a robust cyber-security framework to ensure that e₹ is kept secure in the user’s wallet. The e₹ stored in the e₹ wallet is safe even if the mobile device on which the e₹ wallet is installed is lost. The e₹ wallet can be recovered using the same phone number / SIM on a new mobile device.
Who can use e₹?
All the Union Bank of India account holders having active debit card linked across the country can now register on Digital Rupee by UBI app.
Which mobile operating system platform does e₹ wallet currently support?
e₹ wallet is available and supported both on Android and iOS mobile devices.
What do the e₹ pilots aim to achieve?
The e₹ pilots are a limited scale, controlled roll-out, to test the technology, architecture, scalability, application, features, use-cases and acceptance of e₹. The pilots aim to test the robustness of the entire process of e₹ creation, distribution, usage, etc.
Can Digital Rupee be transacted from one’s bank account outside banking hours?
Yes. e₹ can be loaded/redeemed/transferred from/to one’s bank account to/from the e₹ wallet 24 hours x 7 days a week.
Is there any minimum balance required to open/maintain an e₹ wallet?
No. There is no minimum balance required to open/maintain an e₹ wallet.
Is savings account mandatory for retail customers to use e₹?
Currently, opening of e₹ wallets is linked to user’s saving accounts. This linkage facilitates frictionless user onboarding and obviates the need for separate KYC requirement.
Why do I see e₹ with different denominations in the wallet?
For ease of use, e₹ is available in the same denominations as physical currency. This provides the users with the same familiarity and convenience as with usage of physical currency notes.
How can one transact without having the required denomination or change?
The feature of providing the requisite change is available in all the e₹ wallets. For example, if one desires to purchase a ₹15 item from a merchant but has only ₹20 denomination available in the e₹ wallet, she/he can enter ₹15 in the amount field while sending the money; ₹15 will automatically be credited to the merchant while the balance ₹5 will be returned to the e₹ wallet.
Are there any charges/fees for using e₹?
There are no charges or fees associated with using e₹ or e₹ wallets.
Will interest be paid on the ₹ balances in the e₹ wallet?
Given the cash-like features of e₹, there is no interest payable on the wallet balances.
Whether e₹ is legal tender?
Yes, e₹ is a legal tender.
Is there any difference between e₹ and UPI?
e₹ is a digital form of ₹ whereas UPI is a means of payment. In addition to being used for payments, e₹ also serves as a ‘store of value’, i.e., e₹ can be withdrawn from one’s bank account and kept separately in the e₹ wallet.
In the case of P2P and P2M payments made at any CBDC QR, the transactions happen between two e₹ wallets, and are settled instantaneously without passing through user’s bank accounts.
For P2P and P2M payments, the functionality of scanning a UPI QR from an e₹ app has also been enabled. In such cases, the settlement will be based on the UPI settlement timelines.
Is e₹ same as cryptocurrency such as bitcoin?
No. e₹ is digital form of currency notes and has intrinsic value as it represents a claim on the Reserve Bank’s balance sheet. e₹ is issued by the Reserve Bank and is a legal tender.  Crypto products such as bitcoin on the other hand do not exhibit any features of a currency, do not have any intrinsic value, are not backed by assets and are not issued by a central trusted authority, such as RBI.
How to register/sign up for e₹?
Below are the simple steps to follow to install the app and register for CBDC (e₹).
Download the application through playstore and appstore.
Open the App and select ENTER and proceed with acceptance of TERMS & CONDITIONS.
Go through brief introduction of the App by clicking NEXT tab.
Choose the desired SIM and click on VERIFY SIM.
When the SIM verification status is displayed, the user clicks CONTINUE to move towards the next step.
Verify your device through fingerprint, pattern lock, PIN or face lock.
Enter your profile name and continue by selecting your Bank account.
Set up a 6-digit wallet pin and confirm the pin by re-entering the same input.
Continue your registration by linking your Union Bank of India account by debit card authentication for successful registration.
How can I load money in my e₹ wallet?
User selects the required denominations of e₹ by swiping up and down on the currency shown.
User can view the list of selected e₹ and confirms.
User can choose between UPI or his bank for the transaction.
User obtains information about the chosen transaction option, clicks on load e₹, and then enters an authentication PIN.
As soon as the user inputs their transaction PIN, they are directed to the transaction status screen.
User receives transaction status information.
Tokens will be shown in wallet screen.
How to redeem e₹ from wallet and get money back to my linked bank account?
The user swipes up the token that he wishes to redeem and then selects View e₹ to display the entire amount of tokens swiped.
When the user clicks Redeem e-Rupee after using the swipe token, a bank account selection screen appears.
After choosing a bank account, the user should click the confirm button.
The user can view the specifics of chosen tokens, including the quantity and bank account information.
When the user clicks "Redeem e-Rupee," the transaction requires authentication.
User can able to see the transaction status and transaction details.
How do I transfer to e₹ to another wallet?
User can transfer e₹ by either using VPA, Mobile number or scanning QR code of intended recipient.
The user must select the e₹ denominations or input the desired amount in the edit text field located above the displayed currency and confirms.
When the user clicks on the send e₹ token button, the bottom sheet of the screen displays a confirmation that the amount or tokens have been sent to the intended recipient.
User confirms payee information and amount, then clicks "Send eRupee" to proceed with transaction authentication.
The user enters his six-digit transaction PIN and is then taken to the status screen.
The user can view the status and transaction data.
Users have the ability to share transaction information and report problems by clicking the "Raise Dispute" button.
While loading, what if account debited but wallet not credited with e₹ (Digital rupee)?
In such cases, money will be refunded back to your account. If you do not receive the refund within 48 hours (during pilot phase), please raise a dispute on the e₹ app or contact
customercare@unionbankofindia.bank.in.
How do I make payment to Merchant?
Open e₹ (Digital Rupee) application and click on “Scan and Pay” to initiate payment to merchant. Scan the merchant QR and enter amount. Use Wallet PIN to complete the transaction.
What is Special Purpose e₹ (Digital Rupee) wallet?
Special Purpose Digital Rupee wallet is a sub-type of e₹ (Digital Rupee) wallet which is designed to bifurcate rule-based programmable e₹ (Digital Rupee) received from other users.
Special Purpose Digital Rupee wallet allows the users to track and monitor the usage of rule-based programmable e₹ (Digital Rupee) separately under “View Wallet” section.
Is e₹ (Digital Rupee) mobile application available for merchants?
Yes.
e₹ (Digital Rupee) mobile application is available for merchants.
Load and Redeem features are restricted for merchant application. Merchant users can view the payments received, track and monitor the final settlement of the transactions through the application.
Can I use e₹ via ATM cards or Online Banking?
No. e₹ can only be used with the linked account.
What if I lose/change my phone?
You can recover your wallet by using the same phone number/sim by following the below mentioned steps:
Download the application through playstore and appstore.
Open the App and select ENTER and proceed with acceptance of TERMS & CONDITIONS.
Go through brief introduction of the App by clicking NEXT tab.
Choose the desired SIM and click on VERIFY SIM.
When the SIM verification status is displayed, the user clicks CONTINUE to move towards the next step.
Verify your device through fingerprint, pattern lock, PIN or face lock.
App will display the existing wallet and request to proceed for wallet recovery.
Select wallet and either enter Wallet PIN or choose Forget PIN to recreate new wallet PIN to recover your old e₹ wallet.
Can a rooted device be linked with the wallet?
No. Rooted device cannot be linked with the e₹ wallet.
How to de-register/delete e₹ wallet?
To de-register the e₹ wallet, first redeem the Digital Rupee to your linked bank account.
User initiate the De-registration of Wallet
Provide the App authentication and Wallet Pin to confirm the consent for deregistration.
Wallet will unload the tokens and credit the linked UBI account of user.
Also, the request for the deregistration of device is made.
Once successful, the deregistration process is completed.
Whom I can reach out for support/disputes?
You can raise disputes via e₹ wallet App or customer can reach directly on
customercare@unionbankofindia.bank.in.
Can I use this for investment? Will the value of e₹ appreciate/grow?
No. The value of e₹ is equivalent a physical currency. There is no interest paid on e₹.
How many wallets customer can hold?
Only one e₹ wallet can be held in a particular bank against the registered mobile number.
Technical issues related to Digital rupee (e₹) App?
You can contact Customer Care 1800 2222 44 for enquiries.
What are the transaction velocity limits for Digital Rupee by UBI app?
Following are the transaction velocity limits for Digital Rupee by UBI application:

What is the limit of deregistration on Digital Rupee by UBI app?
A user is allowed to have maximum 3 wallets in a lifetime.

Cards

                Sparsh Braille Card
Sparsh Braille Card
Union Bank of India is pioneer in inclusive banking, and it has designed an innovative product – the Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card. As part of bank’s accessibility integration program and with the help from the disability domain experts, we have made sure, debit card has tactile marks for easy identification, orientation and use. This debit card is a unique solution that caters to the needs of visually impaired customers. The card has Braille features and an innovative design, making it easy to use for visually challenged individuals. As a part of our continued innovative efforts to focus on the needs of the differently abled community and come up with new and innovative solutions to make their banking experience hassle-free we have launched this special Debit Card.
Features of the Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card
Raised Braille dots
One of the most significant features of the Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card is that it has raised Braille dots. These dots are embossed on the card, making it easy for visually impaired individuals to differentiate between other banks' cards.
Braille alphabet 'U'
The Braille alphabet 'U' is appropriately tactile and serves as a quick marker to understand that the card is issued by Union Bank of India. This is an essential feature as it enables the visually impaired to identify the card accurately, preventing them from using the wrong card.
Rounded notch
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card has a rounded notch (half circle) on the opposite side of the chip. It helps customers use the right card, the right way, by touch alone. The notch is a tactile feature that allows customers to use the card without any assistance, making it a hassle-free experience for them.
Glossy Spot UV lamination effect
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card has a glossy spot UV lamination effect with raised texture on the bank's large size of the logo and silk screen rough spot UV on the contactless symbol. This feature helps the visually impaired to locate the bank's logo easily and read the card's contactless symbol, making it easy for them to use the card.
Clutter-free front face
The front face of the Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card is clutter-free, making it easy to feel the Braille and raised texture of the bank logo. This feature ensures that visually impaired individuals can easily locate the necessary information on the card, such as the cardholder's name and card number.
Improved contrasting colours
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card has improved contrasting colours for easier reading. The card is designed in a way that ensures that the colours used on the card are easily distinguishable by visually impaired individuals, making it easy for them to read the card details.
Card details on the back
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card has its card details placed on the back of the card. This feature enables the visually impaired to read the card details independently without any assistance.
NCMC enabled for contactless transactions
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card is NCMC enabled for contactless transactions. This feature is essential as it enables visually impaired individuals to make contactless transactions independently, without any assistance.
Personalized card
The Union Sparsh Braille Debit Card is a personalized card. This means that the card has the cardholder's name and card number embossed on it. This feature ensures that visually impaired individuals can easily identify their card without any assistance.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Are Talking ATMs available at all Union Bank branches?

Can I use Talking ATMs if I have partial vision impairment?
Yes, Talking ATMs are designed to cater to visually impaired individuals, including those with partial vision impairment.
How do I find the Talking ATM option on the ATM screen?
The Talking ATM option can be enabled by inserting an earphone / headphone pin into the ATMs jack. Once the talking (voice guidance) mode is enabled the ATM will guide you through the rest of the transaction using voice instructions.
Can I perform all types of transactions on Talking ATMs?
Yes, you can perform a range of transactions, including cash withdrawals, balance inquiries, fund transfers, and more, on Union Bank's Talking ATMs.
Is there any additional charge for using Talking ATMs?
No, there are no additional charges for using Union Bank's Talking ATMs. Regular transaction fees may apply as per the customer's account type and transaction limits.
Is earphone / headphone made available at the ATM Kiosk?
No, the user need to carry his/her own earphone / headphone to do the Talking ATM transaction.
